message.content:
{"analysis":"I'm seeing two regions: top half is a blue sky gradient (B=255 always), bottom half is a patterned area with values 67 and 152 (grayscale) plus reddish pixels around the center. The bottom half doesn't seem to be a simple regular grid - the vertical pattern varies irregularly. I need to understand the full bottom pattern structure. Let me dump it at lower resolution.","plan":"1. Dump the bottom half at lower resolution to understand the overall pattern shape
2. Map out the reddish region boundaries
3. Look for mathematical relationships","commands":[{"keystrokes":"awk 'NR>3{y=int((NR-4)/2400); x=(NR-4)%2400; if(y>=900 && y%20==0 && x%20==0){r=$1; if(r!=67 && r!=152) printf "(%d,%d): %s\n",y,x,$0}}' /app/image.ppm | head -40
","duration":30.0}]}
